description: Complete vanilla JS calculator with session history
intent: Build a complete calculator as a single-page web app: HTML layout, CSS styling, JS arithmetic engine with operator precedence, button-driven UI, and a session history panel. Each teammate builds their own version independently on their own branch. Best overall implementation wins.
notes: Each person builds in trial-calculator/ on their own branch (feat/task-01-a, feat/task-01-b, feat/task-01-c). Target files: index.html, css/styles.css, js/calculator.js, js/ui.js, js/history.js
acceptance_checks: evaluate('10/3') returns a decimal, not an integer
evaluate('1/0') returns Infinity or throws a clear error, does not crash
Calculator is centered on the page and looks intentional on a typical laptop screen (>=1280px wide)
Buttons have visible hover and active states (color change, shadow, or scale)
Display area has clear visual separation from the button grid
History panel is visually distinct and scrollable if content overflows
No horizontal scrollbar at any viewport >=1024px wide
Clicking digit and operator buttons updates the display in real time
Pressing = evaluates the current expression and shows the result
A Clear button resets the display to empty
Each completed evaluation appears in the history panel (expression and result)
History is in-memory only (clears on page refresh, no localStorage)
Invalid expressions display an error in the result area without crashing the page or breaking the history panel
